data.title: Woodside Energy: 1P reserves 1,882.1 MMboe, 2P reserves 2,999.5 MMboe, 2C resources 5,795.7 MMboe. CEO pleased with portfolio growth.
data.description: Woodside revealed today that as of the end of 2025, they had 1,882.1 million barrels of oil equivalent (MMboe) in remaining proved (1P) reserves, 2,999.5 MMboe in remaining proved plus probable (2P) reserves, and 5,795.7 MMboe in remaining 2C contingent resources. After factoring out divestments and production, proved reserves saw a rise of 134.1 MMboe, while proved plus probable reserves grew by 141.0 MMboe. Acting CEO Liz Westcott noted this increase is due to another year of strong performance from the company's portfolio.
RBA says global growth has proven far more resilient than previously anticipated.
RBA says lending conditions have eased, with banks extending credit readily and credit growth firming.
RBA says financial settings are no longer sufficiently restrictive, despite the stronger Australian dollar.
RBA says downside risks to the labour market have diminished, with conditions still tight and wage costs elevated.
RBA says inflation pressures are widespread and would endure without an additional rate increase.
RBA says a broad set of indicators surprised to the upside and financial conditions have loosened considerably.

data.title: Anderson Cooper is departing his role as a correspondent on ’60 Minutes’ to focus on his CNN show and family.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Mr. Cooper revealed his decision to step down from his role as a correspondent on the show to prioritize his CNN program and increase his family time. This move signifies his intent to dedicate more attention to his children and the CNN program.

data.title: 3D printing industry decline is addressed by FUGO Precision 3D. CEO mentions labor and cost complaints.
data.description: In Gardena, California, on February 16, 2026, Alexander Meseonznik, Co-Founder and CEO of FUGO Precision 3D, commented on the struggles facing the additive manufacturing industry. Meseonznik highlighted ongoing challenges that have led to a decline in the sector, including issues such as excessive labor costs and inefficiencies. The industry has been experiencing a steady decline in recent years, with manufacturers voicing concerns over these persistent issues. Meseonznik stressed the need for solutions to address these problems in order to revitalize the additive manufacturing sector.
